name: Plain White Rice
recipeInstructions:
1. Place two cups of dry rice in a pot with a very well-fitting cover.
2. Wash the rice in cold water, rubbing it between your hands to remove excess starch. Discard the starchy water and rinse the rice again with cold water.
3. Add enough water to cover the rice by a depth of 1/2-3/4 inch (if you prefer fluffier rice, use 3/4 inch; for drier rice, use less).
4. Place the lid on the pot and let the rice stand in the pot for half an hour before cooking.
5. Turn the burner to high heat, place the pot of rice over it, and bring to a full boil. Boil for about 4-5 minutes.
6. Reduce the burner to medium heat and continue boiling for another 5 minutes without lifting the lid, as this allows steam to escape evenly.
7. After cooking on medium heat for 5 minutes, reduce the burner to its lowest setting and allow the rice to steam for an additional 15 minutes or more.
8. Fluff the rice with a large spoon just before serving.
Note: Some water and steam may foam out of the pot during this process; this is normal.
